overview.title: Hosting & Deployment Resources
overview.description: Complete guide to deploying web apps, managing domains, databases, and going from localhost to production with free and paid options

resources.name: Vercel - Frontend Cloud Platform
resources.shortDesc: Deploy Next.js, React, and static sites instantly with global CDN and automatic HTTPS
resources.fullDescription: Vercel is the platform created by the makers of Next.js, optimized for frontend frameworks with zero-configuration deployment. Connect your GitHub repo and every push automatically deploys to production with preview URLs for branches. Features edge functions, analytics, and the world's fastest CDN. Perfect for Next.js, but supports any framework (React, Vue, Angular, Svelte).

features.Custom Domains: Easy domain setup with automatic SSL
features.Rollbacks: Instant rollback to any previous deployment
features.Analytics: Real-time Web Vitals and performance metrics

limitations: Free tier limited to personal projects (no commercial use stated)
Bandwidth overage costs can add up ($0.40/GB)
Function execution time: 10s (Hobby), 60s (Pro)
Primarily frontend-focused (backend needs serverless functions)
Cold starts on serverless functions

deployment.customDomain: Add domain → Configure DNS → Auto SSL

resources.name: Netlify - All-in-One Platform for Modern Web
resources.shortDesc: Deploy static sites and serverless functions with form handling and split testing
resources.fullDescription: Netlify is a comprehensive platform for deploying modern web projects, offering static site hosting, serverless functions, form handling, and A/B testing. Like Vercel but with unique features like form submissions, identity management, and large file uploads. Excellent for Jamstack sites, marketing pages, and content-heavy applications. Free tier is very generous.

features.Edge Functions: Run code at the edge (Deno runtime)

bestFor: Projects with large media files (Git LFS)
Sites requiring form handling without backend
limitations: Build minutes limited on free tier (300/month)
Functions have 10s timeout (26s on paid plans)
Can be expensive for high-traffic sites ($20/100GB)
Bandwidth overages costlier than Vercel
Build times can be slower than Vercel

deployment.envVariables: Set in Site settings → Environment variables
deployment.forms: Add data-netlify='true' to forms (no backend needed)

codeSnippet.title: netlify.toml Configuration
codeSnippet.code:
[build]
  command = "npm run build"
  publish = "dist"
  functions = "netlify/functions"

[build.environment]
  NODE_VERSION = "18"

[[redirects]]
  from = "/api/*"
  to = "/.netlify/functions/:splat"
  status = 200

[[headers]]
  for = "/*"
  [headers.values]
    X-Frame-Options = "DENY"
    X-XSS-Protection = "1; mode=block"
    Content-Security-Policy = "default-src 'self'"

# Form handling example
# Add to your HTML form:
# <form name="contact" method="POST" data-netlify="true">
#   <input type="text" name="name" />
#   <button type="submit">Send</button>
# </form>
# Submissions appear in Netlify dashboard!

resources.name: Railway - Deploy Full-Stack Apps Instantly
resources.shortDesc: Deploy Node.js, Python, Go apps with built-in databases and services
resources.fullDescription: Railway is a modern platform for deploying full-stack applications with zero DevOps overhead. Unlike Vercel/Netlify (frontend-focused), Railway excels at backend services, databases, and Docker containers. Features built-in PostgreSQL, Redis, MySQL, and MongoDB. Pay-as-you-go pricing with $5 free credits monthly. Perfect for APIs, web scrapers, Discord bots, and full-stack apps.

specifications.Regions: US West, US East, EU West (auto-select)

bestFor: Projects needing multiple services (app + DB + Redis)
Side projects and MVPs ($5/month is very affordable)
limitations: $5 free credits run out quickly with databases (careful monitoring needed)
No free tier after credits (unlike Vercel/Netlify)
Can get expensive for high-traffic apps
Limited to 3 regions (vs Vercel's 100+ edge locations)
Costs can be unpredictable without usage caps

deployment.method: Connect GitHub repo, deploy Docker, or use CLI

codeSnippet.title: railway.json Configuration

resources.name: Render - Cloud for Modern Apps
resources.shortDesc: Deploy web services, static sites, databases, and cron jobs with free tier
resources.fullDescription: Render is a unified platform for deploying web apps, APIs, static sites, databases, and background workers. Offers a generous free tier for web services (750 hours/month) and static sites. Better free tier than Railway but with some trade-offs (cold starts, slower builds). Supports Docker, Node.js, Python, Go, Ruby, Elixir, and more. Built-in PostgreSQL and Redis.

specifications.Free Tier: 750 hours/month web services, unlimited static sites
specifications.Auto-scaling: Available on Standard plan and above

features.Free Static Sites: Unlimited free static sites (great for portfolios)

bestFor: Free static sites (better than Netlify/Vercel for pure static)
Low-traffic APIs and web services (free tier)
MVPs and side projects (free tier is great)
limitations: Free tier services spin down after 15 min inactivity (50s cold start)
Free PostgreSQL databases deleted after 90 days
750 hours = ~one service always on, or multiple services part-time
Build times slower than Vercel/Netlify
Cold starts can be slow (30-50 seconds)
Free tier limited to 0.5GB RAM (can cause OOM errors)

deployment.method: Connect Git repo (GitHub, GitLab) or deploy Docker
deployment.database: Create PostgreSQL service, copy connection string

codeSnippet.title: render.yaml Blueprint
codeSnippet.code:
services:
  - type: web
    name: my-api
    env: node
    plan: free
    buildCommand: npm install && npm run build
    startCommand: npm start
    envVars:
      - key: DATABASE_URL
        fromDatabase:
          name: mydb
          property: connectionString
      - key: NODE_ENV
        value: production

  - type: web
    name: frontend
    env: static
    buildCommand: npm run build
    staticPublishPath: ./dist

databases:
  - name: mydb
    plan: free
    databaseName: myapp
    user: myapp

# Free tier strategy:
# - Static sites: Always free
# - One web service: Free (750 hours = 24/7)
# - Database: Free for 90 days, then $7/month
# Total: $0/month (first 90 days), $7/month after

resources.name: Namecheap - Domain Registration
resources.shortDesc: Affordable domain registration starting at $8.88/year with free privacy protection
resources.fullDescription: Namecheap is one of the most popular domain registrars known for competitive pricing, free WhoisGuard privacy protection, and excellent customer support. Register .com, .net, .org, and 400+ TLDs. Includes free email forwarding, DNS management, and SSL certificates. No hidden fees, transparent renewal pricing. Great for beginners and professionals alike.

specifications.Free Features: WhoisGuard privacy protection (1 year free, then $2.88/year)

features.WhoisGuard: Hide personal info from WHOIS database (free 1st year)

bestFor: Freelancers and agencies managing client domains

setupGuide.step1: Search for available domain on Namecheap
setupGuide.step2: Add to cart, enable WhoisGuard (free 1st year)
setupGuide.step4: Go to Domain List → Manage → Advanced DNS
setupGuide.step6: Wait 1-48 hours for DNS propagation (usually <1 hour)
setupGuide.step7: Add domain to Vercel/Netlify and auto-provision SSL

codeSnippet.title: DNS Configuration Examples
codeSnippet.code:
# Vercel DNS Setup (in Namecheap Advanced DNS)
# Type    Host    Value                      TTL
# CNAME   www     cname.vercel-dns.com       Automatic
# A       @       76.76.21.21                Automatic

# Netlify DNS Setup
# Type    Host    Value                      TTL
# CNAME   www     yoursite.netlify.app       Automatic
# A       @       75.2.60.5                  Automatic

# Cloudflare Pages Setup (RECOMMENDED)
# 1. Add site to Cloudflare (free plan)
# 2. Change nameservers in Namecheap to:
#    mary.ns.cloudflare.com
#    todd.ns.cloudflare.com
# 3. In Cloudflare: Add CNAME: www → yoursite.pages.dev
# 4. Page Rule: Redirect root to www (or vice versa)

# Email Forwarding Setup
# 1. Namecheap Dashboard → Email Forwarding
# 2. Add: hello@yourdomain.com → yourgmail@gmail.com
# 3. Wait 10-30 min for activation
# 4. Test by sending email to hello@yourdomain.com

resources.name: Supabase - Open Source Firebase Alternative
resources.shortDesc: Free PostgreSQL database with authentication, storage, and real-time subscriptions
resources.fullDescription: Supabase is an open-source Firebase alternative providing a PostgreSQL database, authentication, file storage, and real-time subscriptions. Perfect for modern web apps, it offers a generous free tier with 500MB database storage, 1GB file storage, and 2GB bandwidth. Includes RESTful API, GraphQL support, and client libraries for JavaScript, Python, and more. Self-hostable for full control.

features.Realtime: Subscribe to database changes via WebSockets
features.Authentication: Email, OAuth (Google, GitHub, etc.), magic links
features.Storage: Upload and serve files with CDN
features.Row Level Security: PostgreSQL RLS for fine-grained access control
features.Dashboard: SQL editor, table editor, API explorer

limitations: Free tier pauses after 7 days inactivity (quick restore)
500MB database limit (free) - upgrade to Pro for 8GB
Limited egress bandwidth on free tier
Cold starts on Edge Functions
Real-time connections limited on free tier
No daily backups on free tier

codeSnippet.title: Next.js + Supabase Setup
